note: Mathlib/Algebra/Module/ZLattice/Basic.lean:435 -- class IsZLattice (K) (L : Submodule ℤ E) [DiscreteTopology L]: Prop where span_top : span K (L : Set E) = ⊤. Exactly the group-theoretic lattice concept: a discrete subgroup of a real (or normed-field) vector space that spans it; docstring confirms 'L is a ℤ-lattice if it is discrete and spans E over K'.
verify_note: decl confirmed at cited line, docstring 'A (full) Z-lattice L of E is a discrete subgroup of E such that L spans E over K' matches; Wikidata Q1096885 description 'subgroup of a real vector space' subclass of 'discrete subgroup' matches exactly under K=R specialization (instIsZLatticeRealSpan witnesses this).

note: Mathlib/Analysis/Convex/KreinMilman.lean:96 -- theorem closure_convexHull_extremePoints (hscomp : IsCompact s) (hAconv : Convex ℝ s): s = closure (convexHull ℝ (extremePoints ℝ s)) [the Krein-Milman theorem]. Choquet theory (integral/barycentric representation of points of a compact convex set by measures on its extreme points) is the infinite-dimensional strengthening of Krein-Milman; Mathlib has only the qualitative Krein-Milman ancestor result, not Choquet's actual representation theorem (no Choquet simplex / representing-measure machinery), so this is a related-but-distinct precursor, not the concept itself.
verify_note: decl confirmed (Krein-Milman theorem, line 96, signature matches); Wikidata Q1076274 describes Choquet theory as a whole discipline ('measures which have support on the extreme points of a convex set'), not this single theorem -- 'related' correctly avoids overclaiming exact.

note: Mathlib/NumberTheory/BernoulliPolynomials.lean:120 -- theorem derivative_bernoulli (k : ℕ): derivative (bernoulli k) = k * bernoulli (k-1). This is exactly the Appell-sequence defining property (p_n' = n·p_{n-1}) proved for the Bernoulli polynomials, a canonical named Appell sequence; Mathlib has no general 'IsAppellSequence' class, only this one concrete instance carrying the defining relation.
verify_note: decl confirmed at line 120, statement derivative(bernoulli k) = k*bernoulli(k-1) matches exactly; Wikidata Q1090038 confirms Appell sequence is a general polynomial-sequence class (subclass of Polynomial sequence) with no IsAppellSequence in Mathlib, so 'special_case' (one witnessing instance) is honest, not overclaimed as exact.

note: Mathlib/ModelTheory/Skolem.lean:126 -- the Downward Löwenheim–Skolem theorem: if s ⊆ M and max(#s, L.card) ≤ κ ≤ #M then M has an elementary substructure containing s of cardinality κ. This is precisely the theorem that generates Skolem's paradox (a countable elementary submodel of ZFC satisfying 'the reals are uncountable'); Mathlib formalizes the underlying theorem but not the paradox/meta-observation itself, hence related rather than exact.
verify_note: decl confirmed at line 126, doubling as documented Downward Lowenheim-Skolem theorem; Wikidata Q1096353 defines the QID as the paradox itself ('countable models of theories that assert existence of uncountable sets'), a meta-observation distinct from the underlying theorem -- 'related' is the honest call, not 'exact'.
